solution_summary: Plan a minimal backend-only fix in `backend/app/db/seat_inventory.py` by correcting `_seat_type_for` so business-class seat columns map to the proper seat types: A/D => `window`, B/C => `aisle`. Add or update focused tests to verify business-class classification and window-preference assignment behavior, while leaving other cabin mappings unchanged.
implementation_steps: Inspect `_seat_type_for` and identify the branch that handles business cabin seat columns.
Update the business-class column-to-seat-type mapping so columns A and D return `window`, and columns B and C return `aisle`.
Confirm that economy/premium or any shared helper paths are not unintentionally altered; preserve existing behavior outside business cabin unless the same helper is intentionally shared.
Add or update tests covering business-class seat-type mapping for all four columns (A, B, C, D).
Add an integration-style or preference-assignment test that requests a window seat in business class and verifies the assigned seat is a true window seat, not a center/aisle-adjacent seat.
Run the relevant backend test subset and any lint/static checks used by the repository for seat inventory logic.
Review for regressions in any downstream seat selection or reconciliation logic that consumes `_seat_type_for`.
Prepare a small, targeted PR describing the business-class seat classification correction and test coverage.

planned_changes.change_summary: Fix the business-cabin seat-type mapping in `_seat_type_for` so columns A/D are classified as `window` and B/C as `aisle`.
planned_changes.rationale: This is the explicit source of the bug and the smallest safe fix location.

planned_changes.file_path: backend/app/db/seat_inventory.py (tests or adjacent test module, if present)
planned_changes.change_summary: Add/adjust unit tests for `_seat_type_for` covering business-cabin columns A through D and asserting unchanged behavior for other cabins if applicable.
planned_changes.rationale: Prevents the mapping regression from reappearing and validates the exact column semantics required by acceptance criteria.

planned_changes.file_path: backend/app/db/seat_inventory.py (integration or booking preference test, if present)
planned_changes.change_summary: Add/adjust a seat-preference test that requests a business-class window seat and verifies assignment to a window column.
planned_changes.rationale: Confirms the user-visible symptom is fixed, not just the classification helper.

patch_strategy: Apply a surgical, localized change to the seat classification helper rather than touching booking logic or schema. First, inspect the existing branch structure in `_seat_type_for` to determine whether the business cabin uses its own mapping or shares logic with other cabins. Then adjust only the business-cabin mapping constants/conditions so A and D resolve to `window` and B and C to `aisle`. Keep non-business behavior intact by preserving existing branches for other cabin types. In parallel, add tests that directly assert the corrected business-class column mapping and a preference-level test that demonstrates a window request now selects a true window seat. Limit the scope to the identified file and nearby tests to avoid unintended side effects.
validation_steps: Run unit tests targeting seat inventory classification logic.
Run any tests covering seat assignment by preference in business class.
If available, run the full backend test module that exercises `_seat_type_for` and related seat inventory behavior.
Verify that test cases for other cabin types still pass, confirming no collateral mapping changes.
Manually review the updated branch logic for business cabin columns A/B/C/D against the acceptance criteria.
pr_title: SDLC-33: Fix business-class seat-type mapping for window preferences
pr_body:
## Summary
Fix business-class seat-type classification so window preferences are assigned to true window seats.

## What changed
- Corrected `_seat_type_for` business-cabin mapping in `backend/app/db/seat_inventory.py`
- Ensured columns A and D are treated as `window`
- Ensured columns B and C are treated as `aisle`
- Added/updated tests to cover business-class seat mapping and preference assignment

## Why
Passengers requesting a window seat in business class were being assigned a center/incorrect seat due to reversed seat-type labels.

## Validation
- Targeted seat inventory tests
- Business-class window preference assignment tests

## Risk
Low: localized fix to seat classification logic only.
risks: If `_seat_type_for` is shared by multiple cabin types, an overly broad change could affect other cabin mappings.
Existing tests may not cover all cabin/column combinations, so a regression check is important.
Downstream seat-selection logic may assume the previous incorrect labels in edge cases, though the issue indicates the current behavior is undesired.
assumptions: `backend/app/db/seat_inventory.py` contains the authoritative seat-type logic for business cabins.
A focused test suite exists or can be added near the seat inventory or booking preference code.
No schema or data migration is needed because this is a runtime classification bug.
